ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flag from vision pass; general trivia harvest only.

trivia_bullets.text: Aetherdrift (set code DFT, released 2025-02-14) is built around the Ghirapur Grand Prix, a death-defying interplanar race for the Aetherspark, an artificial planeswalker spark created by an Avishkari inventor during the New Phyrexian Invasion; the race crosses three planes — Amonkhet, Avishkar, and Muraganda [Scryfall set page; magic.wizards.com Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift Part 1].
sources: magic.wizards.com: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift Part 1

trivia_bullets.text: Muraganda is the Aetherdrift leg most relevant to this card — a primordial plane teased for 17 years before its first set appearance, inhabited by dinosaurs, oozes, and other prehistoric beings, with the Grand Prix running along a specially built elevated track above the proteogenic jungles [MTG Rocks: 'MTG Plane Teased For 17 Years Confirmed As Third Aetherdrift Location'].
sources: mtgrocks.com: Muraganda confirmed as third Aetherdrift location

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "As it digested the demonic roadster, it too learned the thrill of speed." The "demonic roadster" reference points at the Speed Demons, the Duskmourn racing team of possessed drivers and specter-haunted vehicles led by Winter under the archdemon Valgavoth's compulsion — so the ooze in the art has eaten one of their cars off the track [Scryfall flavor_text; MTG Wiki: Speed Demons].

Oracle text: "When this creature enters, mill two cards, then put a +1/+1 counter on this creature for each artifact and/or creature card in your graveyard." Draftsim's Aetherdrift Limited review notes Ooze Patrol "can be quite large in the right deck, but if you just cast it empty, it's a Hill Giant on average, so you should try to do some trading or self-milling early before playing it" — pairs with the set's black-green graveyard/reanimator archetype [Draftsim: Ultimate Aetherdrift Limited Set Review].
sources: draftsim.com: Aetherdrift Limited Set Review (via WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: Art by Forrest Schehl, who illustrated three cards in Aetherdrift [Scryfall; mtg.wtf artist page (via WebSearch snippet)]. The card is uncommon, mana cost {3}{G}, 2/2 base body, EDHREC rank 19255 at time of writing (low Commander adoption) [Scryfall].

Aetherdrift was described by graveyard-archetype writers as "secretly stacked with great graveyard support," with Ooze Patrol sitting alongside enablers like Molt Tender, Dredger's Insight, and Quag Feast in the green self-mill suite that feeds the BG reanimator pile [MTG Rocks: 'MTG Aetherdrift Is Secretly Stacked With Great Graveyard Support' (via WebSearch snippet)].
sources: mtgrocks.com: Aetherdrift graveyard support (via WebSearch snippet)

related_cards.name: Terrian, World Tyrant
related_cards.note: Legendary Dinosaur Ooze (no. 182), 9/7 for {2}{G}{G}{G}; Muraganda's signature ooze, described in flavor as having consumed entire ecosystems and keeping them suspended in its core — the apex version of the consumed-thing-inside-the-ooze visual gag Ooze Patrol does in miniature.

related_cards.name: Mimeoplasm, Revered One
related_cards.note: Legendary Sultai Ooze (no. 214); shares the consume-a-creature-and-take-its-power conceit; the mythic ooze of the set.

related_cards.name: Slick Imitator
related_cards.note: Uncommon blue ooze (no. 62) with Start Your Engines!; the other booster-common-cycle ooze that participates directly in the racing mechanic.

related_cards.name: Molt Tender
related_cards.note: Green uncommon self-mill enabler called out as a top Aetherdrift graveyard piece; feeds Ooze Patrol's +1/+1 counter trigger before it enters.

related_cards.name: Quag Feast
related_cards.note: Green self-mill removal spell; rounds out the green mill suite that turns Ooze Patrol into a payoff rather than a vanilla 2/2.
